title: 10 best herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy are plant-based remedies that help alleviate symptoms of an enlarged prostate. They work by reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow. The best herbal essential oils for this condition include Serenoa repens, Urtica dioica, Curcuma longa, Pygeum africanum, Ginkgo biloba, Prunus africana, Zingiber officinale, Cucurbita pepo, Sabal serrulata, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, benign prostatic hypertrophy can lead to urinary incontinence, painful urination, and even kidney damage, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it targets the key underlying issue of DHT conversion, which is a primary contributor to enlarged prostate tissue. The oil's active ingredients, specifically beta-carotene and fatty acids, have been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT). By reducing DHT levels, saw palmetto essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of BPH such as frequent urination, weak stream, and prostate discomfort.
remedy_properties: 5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's fatty acid and sterol constituents can inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH symptoms, thus reducing prostate size and alleviating urinary tract issues.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The extract may help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which is often associated with BPH, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids and enzymes.

Androgen receptor modulation: Saw palmetto's constituents may modulate androgen receptors, potentially reducing the binding of DHT to these receptors and thereby decreasing the stimulation of prostate growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in fatty acids and phytosterols, which help to regulate hormone imbalances and reduce inflammation associated with BPH.
Fruits: The fruits contain beta-sitosterol, a natural compound that has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is linked to BPH.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh serenoa repens leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried off for better oil yield.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation at room temperature or by spreading thin layers on paper bags for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation using a mortar and pestle.
Perform steam distillation of the crushed plant material with 1:2 ratio of plant material to water at 100°c for 30-40 minutes.
Collect the extracted essential oil in a clean glass container and store it in an airtight bottle in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: African plum essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as beta-sitosterol and lupeol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms like urinary frequency and nocturia associated with BPH. Additionally, the essential oil's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue helps to ease bladder obstruction and improve urine flow, providing relief for men suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Pygeum's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as isoflavones and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which is a common characteristic of BPH.
Alpha-blocker activity: The bark of Pygeum africanum contains compounds that have been shown to relax smooth muscle in the prostate and urethra, improving urine flow and reducing symptoms of BPH.

Antioxidant activity: Pygeum's antioxidant properties may help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of P. africana contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat urinary tract problems and prostatic issues, including BPH.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of P. africana are rich in phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them suitable for treating BPH symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of prunus africana bark in the morning after it has been dried for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried prunus africana bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for approximately 15 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered prunus africana bark with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a dark place at room temperature for 14 days, shaking it gently once every day to facilitate the extraction process.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the infused carrier oil to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease urinary symptoms associated with BPH. The oil's ability to relax muscles and calm the prostate gland may also help alleviate urinary frequency, nocturia, and incomplete bladder emptying. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant properties may help protect against cellular damage and promote overall prostate health, making it a potentially effective natural remedy for managing BPH symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of BPH such as urinary frequency and urgency.

Alpha-blocker activity: The oil's alpha-blocking compounds help relax smooth muscle tissues in the prostate gland, allowing for improved urine flow and reducing symptoms of BPH, such as hesitancy and weak stream.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging: Stinging nettle essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are ideal for producing essential oils that help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Seeds: Seeds contain a high concentration of fatty acids and antioxidants, making them an excellent choice for creating therapeutic essential oils that support prostate health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves by cutting them from the plant in early morning dew for optimal potency retention.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours or until crumbly texture is achieved.
Steam distill the dried nettle leaves with distilled water at a ratio of 1:5 for 4 hours to extract essential oil.
Separate and filter the extracted essential oil from the distillation condensate using a coffee filter paper and store in dark glass bottles.
Combine the filtered essential oil with jojoba oil at a ratio of 10% nettle oil to 90% jojoba oil, shaking well before storing in refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of camellia sinensis and wash them gently in cold running water for 30 seconds.
Steam distill the washed camellia sinensis leaves at 100°c for 3 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine distilled essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3 drops per ml.
Store the prepared herbal essential oil in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.
Apply the prepared herbal essential oil topically three times a day, massaging gently into prostate area for 2 minutes each application.

remedy_recipe: Harvest freshly grown betula pendula leaves from a sustainable source.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and stems in the early morning when dew is still present on the plants weighing around 100 grams.

Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass container with a cap tightly sealed after separating it from the water and plant residue.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment for about 15 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark amber glass bottles with tight-fitting caps at room temperature away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
